message:
chore: code review cleanup

- Remove unused GH_CALL_LOG variable from test_e2e_apply_phase.sh
- Add function existence check for find_repo_spec_for_repo_id in ru

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -296,7 +296,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-i1jw","title":"Real unit tests for URL parsing functions","description":"Test URL parsing without mocks using real string operations.

Functions to test:
- parse_repo_url(): Handle git@, https://, ssh:// URLs
- parse_repo_spec(): Parse owner/repo, full URLs, local paths
- normalize_url(): Standardize URL formats
- url_to_local_path(): Convert URLs to filesystem paths
- url_to_clone_target(): Get clone target from URL

Test cases:
- All URL schemes (https, git@, ssh)
- Edge cases (trailing slashes, .git suffix)
- Invalid URLs (proper error handling)
- Local path handling

No mocks needed - pure string manipulation tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.099743032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:05:59.886908371-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:05:59.886908371-05:00","close_reason":"Added 30 unit tests for normalize_url, url_to_local_path, and url_to_clone_target.  {"id":"bd-i6mc","title":"Fix state lock eval + unsafe RU_STATE_DIR","description":"acquire_state_lock used eval to open lock fd; write_json_atomic used echo; and several review-state paths accepted relative/unsafe RU_STATE_DIR/XDG_STATE_HOME which could create directories under CWD. Remove eval, use printf, and harden path selection to absolute/tilde paths only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:28.783651473-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:46.79692854-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:46.79692854-05:00","close_reason":"Removed eval from acquire_state_lock (uses exec {fd}); switched write_json_atomic to printf; normalized state-dir base selection to avoid relative/CWD writes"}
-{"id":"bd-i99q","title":"Testing: Integration Tests for Review Orchestration","description":"# Testing: Integration Tests for Review Orchestration

## Objective
Create comprehensive integration and end-to-end tests for the entire review orchestration system. These tests verify that all components work together correctly.

## Test Summary

### Unit Test Suites (Total: 114 tests)
| File | Tests | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| test_unit_worktree.sh | 12 | Worktree creation, mapping, digest cache |
| test_unit_session_driver.sh | 8 | Driver detection, session lifecycle |
| test_unit_session_monitor.sh | 14 | State detection, hysteresis, wait reasons |
| test_unit_question_tui.sh | 12 | Queue, context, drill-down, routing |
| test_unit_apply_phase.sh | 10 | Validation, quality gates, gh_actions |
| test_unit_completion.sh | 8 | Outcomes, digest cache, reports |
| test_unit_rate_governor.sh | 10 | Rate limits, circuit breaker |
| test_unit_checkpoint.sh | 10 | State persistence, resume |
| test_unit_orchestration.sh | 16 | Args, budgets, prefetch |
| test_unit_metrics.sh | 6 | Aggregation, reporting |
| test_unit_discovery.sh | 8 | GraphQL batching, priority scoring (existing) |

### E2E Test Suites (Total: 38 tests)
| File | Tests | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| test_e2e_worktree.sh | 4 | Lifecycle, parallel, recovery, digest roundtrip |
| test_e2e_session_driver.sh | 3 | Full cycle, parallel, interrupt |
| test_e2e_session_monitor.sh | 4 | Monitoring, stall recovery, rate limits |
| test_e2e_question_tui.sh | 4 | Full flow, parallel, persistence, drill-down |
| test_e2e_apply_phase.sh | 3 | Full cycle, quality failure, dry-run |
| test_e2e_completion.sh | 3 | Full cycle, partial, resume-after |
| test_e2e_rate_governor.sh | 3 | Lifecycle, rate limit recovery, circuit breaker |
| test_e2e_checkpoint.sh | 3 | Full resume, questions, no duplicate |
| test_e2e_orchestration.sh | 6 | Full cycle, questions, parallel, interrupt, budgets |
| test_e2e_metrics.sh | 2 | Recording, accumulation |
| test_e2e_discovery.sh | 3 | GraphQL batching, priority (existing) |

### Integration Test Suite (5+ tests)
test_integration_review.sh - Full system integration

## Test Framework

### Mock Infrastructure
```bash
# test/mocks/gh_mock.sh
setup_gh_mock() {
    export GH_MOCK_DIR="$BATS_TMPDIR/gh_mock"
    mkdir -p "$GH_MOCK_DIR/responses"

    cat > "$GH_MOCK_DIR/gh" << 'GHEOF'
#!/bin/bash
case "$*" in
    "api rate_limit")
        cat "$GH_MOCK_DIR/responses/rate_limit.json"
        ;;
    "api graphql"*)
        cat "$GH_MOCK_DIR/responses/graphql.json"
        ;;
    "issue list"*)
        cat "$GH_MOCK_DIR/responses/issues.json"
        ;;
    "auth status")
        exit 0
        ;;
    "issue comment"*|"pr comment"*)
        echo "Mock: $*" >> "$GH_MOCK_DIR/calls.log"
        ;;
    "issue close"*|"pr close"*)
        echo "Mock: $*" >> "$GH_MOCK_DIR/calls.log"
        ;;
    *)
        echo "Mock: unknown: $*" >> "$GH_MOCK_DIR/calls.log"
        ;;
esac
GHEOF
    chmod +x "$GH_MOCK_DIR/gh"
    export PATH="$GH_MOCK_DIR:$PATH"
}
```

### Test Fixtures
```
test/fixtures/
+-- claude_stream/
|   +-- basic_session.ndjson
|   +-- ask_user_question.ndjson
|   +-- external_prompt.ndjson
|   +-- error_session.ndjson
|   +-- rate_limit_429.ndjson
+-- gh/
|   +-- graphql_batch_response.json
|   +-- rate_limit_response.json
|   +-- rate_limit_low.json
|   +-- issue_list.json
+-- plans/
|   +-- simple_fix.json
|   +-- multiple_items.json
|   +-- with_questions.json
|   +-- failed_tests.json
+-- repos/
|   +-- simple-bug/
|   +-- with-pr/
|   +-- dirty-tree/
+-- digests/
    +-- cached_digest.md
    +-- cached_digest.meta.json
```

### Golden Plan Artifacts
```bash
@test "review produces expected plan for fixture repo" {
    ru review --mode=local --repos="fixture/simple-bug" --plan

    local plan_file
    plan_file=$(find "$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees" -name "review-plan.json" | head -1)

    # Compare (ignoring timestamps and SHAs)
    local actual expected
    actual=$(jq 'del(.run_id, .metadata.started_at, .metadata.completed_at, .git.commits[].sha)' "$plan_file")
    expected=$(jq 'del(.run_id, .metadata.started_at, .metadata.completed_at, .git.commits[].sha)' "test/golden/simple-bug-plan.json")

    [[ "$actual" == "$expected" ]]
}
```

## Integration Test Cases

### test_integration_review.sh
```bash
@test "INT: full review cycle with mock GitHub" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 3

    run ru review --mode=local --dry-run
    assert_success
    assert_output --partial "3 work items"

    run ru review --mode=local --plan
    assert_success

    # Verify worktrees created
    assert [ -d "$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees" ]

    # Verify plans created
    local plans
    plans=$(find "$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees" -name "review-plan.json" | wc -l)
    assert [ "$plans" -eq 3 ]

    # Verify all plans valid
    find "$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees" -name "review-plan.json" | while read -r plan; do
        run validate_review_plan "$plan"
        assert_success
    done
}

@test "INT: apply phase executes gh actions" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 1

    ru review --mode=local --plan
    ru review --apply --dry-run

    # Verify gh commands would be called
    assert [ -f "$GH_MOCK_DIR/calls.log" ]
    assert grep -q "issue comment" "$GH_MOCK_DIR/calls.log"
}

@test "INT: resume continues from checkpoint" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 5

    # Start and interrupt
    timeout 10 ru review --mode=local --plan || true

    # Verify state saved
    assert [ -f "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json" ]

    # Resume
    run ru review --resume
    assert_success
}

@test "INT: parallel limit respected" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 10

    ru review --mode=local --plan --parallel=2 &
    local pid=$!
    sleep 5

    local active
    active=$(pgrep -f "claude -p" | wc -l || echo 0)
    assert [ "$active" -le 2 ]

    kill $pid 2>/dev/null || true
}

@test "INT: rate limit triggers backoff" {
    setup_gh_mock
    echo '{"resources":{"core":{"remaining":100,"reset":9999999999}}}' > "$GH_MOCK_DIR/responses/rate_limit.json"

    setup_test_repos 3

    ru review --mode=local --plan &
    local pid=$!
    sleep 10

    local status
    status=$(get_governor_status)
    local parallelism
    parallelism=$(echo "$status" | jq '.parallelism')

    assert [ "$parallelism" -le 2 ]

    kill $pid 2>/dev/null || true
}

@test "INT: question flow roundtrip" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repo_with_question

    ru review --mode=local --plan &
    local pid=$!
    sleep 5

    # Verify question queued
    assert [ -f "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json" ]

    # Answer question
    answer_first_question "option_a"

    wait $pid || true

    # Verify answer processed
    local answered
    answered=$(jq '[.[] | select(.answered == true)] | length' "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-questions.json")
    assert [ "$answered" -gt 0 ]
}

@test "INT: digest cache improves second run" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 1

    # First run - no cache
    local start1=$(date +%s)
    ru review --mode=local --plan
    local duration1=$(( $(date +%s) - start1 ))

    # Cleanup but keep cache
    rm -rf "$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees"

    # Second run - with cache
    local start2=$(date +%s)
    ru review --mode=local --plan
    local duration2=$(( $(date +%s) - start2 ))

    # Second run should have cache loaded
    assert grep -q "Loaded cached digest" "$RU_STATE_DIR/logs/review.log"
}

@test "INT: cost budget max-repos enforced" {
    setup_gh_mock
    setup_test_repos 10

    run ru review --mode=local --plan --max-repos=3
    assert_success

    local processed
    processed=$(jq '[.repos | to_entries[] | select(.value.status == "completed")] | length' "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json")
    assert [ "$processed" -le 3 ]
}
```

## Test Execution

### Run All Tests
```bash
./scripts/run_all_tests.sh --include-integration
```

### Run Specific Suite
```bash
./scripts/run_all_tests.sh --filter="test_unit_worktree"
```

### Run with Coverage
```bash
./scripts/run_all_tests.sh --coverage
```

### Parallel Execution
```bash
./scripts/run_all_tests.sh -j 4
```

## Logging in Tests

All tests produce detailed logs:
```bash
log_test_start() {
    local test_name="$1"
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] TEST START: $test_name" >> "$TEST_LOG"
}

log_test_step() {
    local step="$1"
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)]   STEP: $step" >> "$TEST_LOG"
}

log_test_result() {
    local result="$1"
    local duration="$2"
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] TEST END: $result (${duration}ms)" >> "$TEST_LOG"
}

# Capture all ru output in tests
capture_ru_output() {
    local cmd="$*"
    local output_file="$TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR/ru-output-$(date +%s).log"
    RU_LOG_LEVEL=debug "$@" 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
}
```

## CI Integration

### GitHub Actions Workflow
```yaml
name: Tests
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Run tests
        run: ./scripts/run_all_tests.sh
      - name: Upload test artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: test/artifacts/
          retention-days: 14
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 114 unit tests implemented and passing
- [ ] 38 e2e tests implemented and passing
- [ ] 7+ integration tests implemented and passing
- [ ] Mock infrastructure for gh commands
- [ ] Test fixtures for all scenarios
- [ ] Golden artifacts for regression testing
- [ ] Detailed logging in all tests
- [ ] CI integration (run_all_tests.sh)
- [ ] Coverage tracking

 {"id":"bd-ircy","title":"Define phase prompts with structured output markers","description":"# Phase Prompts with Structured Output

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Define the three phase prompts that produce machine-parseable JSON output.

## Phase 1 Prompt (Understanding)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT='First read AGENTS.md (if present) and README.md (if present) carefully.
If a file is missing, explicitly note that and continue.
Then use your investigation mode to understand the codebase architecture,
entrypoints, conventions, and what the current changes appear to be.
At the end, output a short structured summary as JSON between:
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_BEGIN
{ "summary": "...", "conventions": [...], "risks": [...], "notes": [...] }
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_END'
```

## Phase 2 Prompt (Commit Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT='Now, based on your knowledge of the project, DO NOT run git commands.
Instead, produce a COMMIT PLAN as JSON between these markers:
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END

Rules:
- Do not edit any code or files.
- Do not include ephemeral/ignored files (.pyc, node_modules, __pycache__, etc.).
- Group changes into logically connected commits.
- For each commit, include:
  - "files": explicit list of paths to stage
  - "message": full commit message (subject + body)
- Include "push": true/false
- Include "excluded_files": list of files excluded and why
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Phase 3 Prompt (Release Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT='If a release is warranted based on the changes, DO NOT execute release commands.
Produce a RELEASE PLAN as JSON between:
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_END

Include:
- "version": proposed version (or null if no release needed)
- "tag": proposed tag (or null)
- "changelog_entry": text to add to CHANGELOG
- "version_files": files to update with new version
- "checks": actions to verify before release (tests/CI)
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Commit Plan Schema
```json
{
  "commits": [
    {"files": ["path/a", "path/b"], "message": "feat(x): summary\n\nBody..."},
    {"files": ["path/c"], "message": "fix(y): summary\n\nBody..."}
  ],
  "push": true,
  "excluded_files": [
    {"path": "__pycache__/foo.pyc", "reason": "bytecode cache"}
  ],
  "assumptions": ["No breaking changes detected"],
  "risks": ["Large diff in core module"]
}
```

## Release Plan Schema
```json
{
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "tag": "v1.2.0",
  "changelog_entry": "## v1.2.0 (2026-01-06)\n\n### Added\n- ...",
  "version_files": [
    {"path": "VERSION", "old": "1.1.0", "new": "1.2.0"}
  ],
  "checks": ["tests", "lint"]
}
```

## Environment Variable Overrides
```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 1
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 2
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 3
```

## Per-Repo Prompt Files
Check for: $REPO_PATH/.ru/phase{1,2,3}-prompt.txt
If exists, use file contents instead of default.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:51:33.295772184-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:52:20.035151713-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:52:20.035151713-05:00","close_reason":"Phase prompts implemented by BlueRaven - AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE{1,2,3}_PROMPT constants and get_effective_phase_prompt() function with per-repo override support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ircy","depends_on_id":"bd-6nuc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T18:03:19.896537113-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
